message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

content.content:
68980db6 feat: Google OAuth direct auth + Convex send-email API + Unipile refactor
1ad87581 feat: full outbound sequencer with 2-mailbox MVP limit
726c4442 feat(P4): Background execution — cron dispatcher + reply webhook
ec3a09d7 feat(P3): Send flow via Convex→Unipile with intent→confirm pattern
71d56e4d feat(P2): Convex schema + sync functions + Unipile hosted auth + backfill
15130d77 feat(P1): SQLite schema + sync engine + CLI pull/push commands
666aa675 docs: major spec revision — Convex authority, Unipile hosted auth, send intent pattern
e804d771 docs: address second review — missing tables in sync scope, phase sequence_drafts creation
e48a3f4d docs: address spec review — fix sync conflicts, add token lifecycle, retry strategy, security
4ec3d7b3 docs: add Convex + Unipile sequencer background execution design spec

input.description: Show diffs for schema and sync

content.content:
diff --git a/convex/schema.ts b/convex/schema.ts
index 838c45c6..ebe8c7df 100644
--- a/convex/schema.ts
+++ b/convex/schema.ts
@@ -273,6 +273,23 @@ export default defineSchema({
     .index("by_user", ["userId"])
     .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"]),
 
+  // ── Context metrics (SQLite authoritative, daily rollups pushed) ──
+
+  contextMetricsDaily: defineTable({
+    externalId: v.string(),     // "2026-03-25:emails list"
+    day: v.string(),            // "2026-03-25"
+    command: v.string(),        // "emails list"
+    calls: v.float64(),
+    totalBytes: v.float64(),
+    totalTokens: v.float64(),
+    syncVersion: v.float64(),
+    userId: v.string(),
+  })
+    .index("by_user", ["userId"])
+    .index("by_external_id", ["externalId"])
+    .index("by_day", ["userId", "day"])
+    .index("by_command", ["userId", "command"]),
+
   // ── Convex-only tables ───────────────────────────────────────────
 
   unipileAccounts: defineTable({
diff --git a/internal/sync/sync.go b/internal/sync/sync.go
index 903feda1..da7dbbf6 100644
--- a/internal/sync/sync.go
+++ b/internal/sync/sync.go
@@ -30,6 +30,7 @@ var localTables = []string{
 	"learnings",
 	"skill_versions",
 	"deals",
+	"context_metrics_daily",
 }
 
 const batchSize = 500

content.content:
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-1-foundation.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-1-foundation.md
index 0dfbc96b..9befabe1 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-1-foundation.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-1-foundation.md
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ Use their name naturally throughout the rest of setup.
 
 Ask: "What's your website?"
 
-Use WebFetch to scrape their site. Extract: what they sell, market/industry, positioning, company size signals, and social proof (customer logos, testimonials, metrics, case study results, notable clients, press mentions).
+Run `nurge scrape <url>` to fetch their site. This returns structured JSON with title, description, headings, content, social proof, and links — much more context-efficient than WebFetch. Extract: what they sell, market/industry, positioning, company size signals, and social proof.
 
 Present back: "[Name], here's what I found about your company: [summary]. Does this look right?"
 
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@ Let the user confirm or correct. Save key details:
 - `nurge config set company.description "<one-line>"`
 - `nurge config set company.industry "<industry>"`
 
-**If WebFetch fails** (site down, blocked, no useful content): Ask the user to describe their company in 2-3 sentences. Save to config. Skill generation uses description instead of scrape.
+**If scrape fails** (site down, blocked, no useful content): Ask the user to describe their company in 2-3 sentences. Save to config. Skill generation uses description instead of scrape.
 
 ---
 
@@ -90,12 +90,17 @@ If user declines, respect that. Set `detection.outbound_count 0` and proceed wit
 If user consents:
 
 1. Run `nurge sync` — pulls last 90 days of sent mail.
-2. Classify ALL emails in batches — this is a full classification, not a quick count.
-   - Run `nurge emails list --format json --class unclassified` and classify in batches.
+2. **Auto-classify first:** Run `nurge emails classify-auto --format json` — this uses heuristics to handle obvious cases (internal=same domain, transactional=noreply, newsletter=unsubscribe signals, personal=personal-to-personal). Typically handles 60-70% of emails with zero context cost.
+3. **Classify remainder with AI in batches:**
+   - Run `nurge emails list --format json --class unclassified --compact --limit 50` to get the next batch.
+   - The `--compact` flag returns only id, subject, from, snippet (4 fields instead of 11) to save context.
+   - The `--limit 50` caps each batch at 50 emails.
    - Categories: outbound, internal, personal, transactional, newsletter.
+   - Pipe classifications back: `echo '[...]' | nurge emails classify`
+   - Repeat until `nurge emails list --class unclassified --compact --limit 50` returns empty.
    - For uncertain ones (~5%), ask the user.
    - Show only summary counts, not raw tables.
-3. Save: `nurge config set detection.outbound_count <number>`
+4. Save: `nurge config set detection.outbound_count <number>` (get count from `nurge emails stats --format json`)
 
 The classified data is reused by Phase 2 flows — they just present the results, they don't re-classify.
 
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-experimenting.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-experimenting.md
index 1abcd4d8..23ab61bf 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-experimenting.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-experimenting.md
@@ -241,11 +241,13 @@ Save: `nurge config set analytics.gap_analysis true`
 
 ---
 
-## E14: Generate Skills
+## E14: Generate Core Skills
 
-Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate all 16 project-local skills.
+Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate the **5 core skills** (product, icp, personas, outbound, pipeline) using `nurge skills write --stdin`.
 
-Skills are hybrid-derived — email data + conversation. Flag this: "These skills are built from your email patterns and our conversation. They'll sharpen as you use Nurge more."
+The remaining 11 skills are generated lazily on first use — this saves context and produces better skills later when there's more data.
+
+Skills are hybrid-derived — email data + conversation. Flag this: "I've set up your core skills from your email patterns and our conversation. Other skills like research, sequences, and analytics will be created when you first use them — they'll be sharper with more data."
 
 ---
 
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-operating.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-operating.md
index 6f3876f1..9489027a 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-operating.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-operating.md
@@ -221,11 +221,13 @@ Save: `nurge config set analytics.gap_analysis true`
 
 ---
 
-## O12: Generate Skills
+## O12: Generate Core Skills
 
-Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate all 16 project-local skills.
+Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate the **5 core skills** (product, icp, personas, outbound, pipeline) using `nurge skills write --stdin`.
 
-Skills are data-derived — immediately strong. Flag this: "These skills are built from your actual email patterns. They're already sharp because you have real data."
+The remaining 11 skills are generated lazily on first use.
+
+Skills are data-derived — immediately strong. Flag this: "I've set up your core skills from your actual email patterns. Other skills like analytics, sequences, and review will be created when you first use them — they're already sharp because you have real data."
 
 ---
 
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-scaling.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-scaling.md
index 56ffc63e..d179a287 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-scaling.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-scaling.md
@@ -228,13 +228,15 @@ Save: `nurge config set analytics.gap_analysis true`
 
 ---
 
-## S13: Generate Skills
+## S13: Generate Core Skills
 
-Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate all 16 project-local skills.
+Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate the **5 core skills** (product, icp, personas, outbound, pipeline) using `nurge skills write --stdin`.
+
+The remaining 11 skills are generated lazily on first use.
 
 Skills are data-derived + team-aware. They should reference team structure, territories, and shared playbooks.
 
-Flag this: "These skills are built for your team. They reference your team structure and use cross-rep patterns."
+Flag this: "I've set up your core skills for the team. Other skills like analytics, review, and coaching will be created when you first use them — they'll reference your team structure and cross-rep patterns."
 
 ---
 
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md
index aaf191d5..17436461 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/phase-2-zero.md
@@ -192,11 +192,13 @@ Save:
 
 ---
 
-## Z11: Generate Skills
+## Z11: Generate Core Skills
 
-Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate all 16 project-local skills.
+Read `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/skill-generation.md` and follow it to generate the **5 core skills** (product, icp, personas, outbound, pipeline) using `nurge skills write --stdin`.
 
-Skills are conversation-derived — they'll improve as data accumulates. Flag this to the user: "These skills are based on what we discussed. As you send more emails and Nurge learns, they'll get sharper."
+The remaining 11 skills are generated lazily on first use — this saves context and produces better skills later when there's more data.
+
+Skills are conversation-derived — they'll improve as data accumulates. Flag this to the user: "I've set up your core skills based on what we discussed. Other skills like research, sequences, and analytics will be created when you first use them — they'll be sharper with more data."
 
 ---
 
diff --git a/internal/project/skills/setup/skill-generation.md b/internal/project/skills/setup/skill-generation.md
index a3ecabc7..afbc33b4 100644
--- a/internal/project/skills/setup/skill-generation.md
+++ b/internal/project/skills/setup/skill-generation.md
@@ -1,19 +1,32 @@
 # Skill Generation Guide
 
-This guide is used by all Phase 2 flows to generate the 16 project-local skills. Read this file when you reach the skill generation step (Z11, E14, O12, or S13).
+This guide is used by all Phase 2 flows to generate skills at setup (Z11, E14, O12, or S13).
 
-## File Structure
+## Context Efficiency
+
+**Generate only the 5 core skills at setup.** The rest are generated lazily on first use — this saves ~15,000 tokens of context during setup and produces better skills later (more data to personalize from).
+
+**Use `nurge skills write --stdin`** to write all skills in one CLI call instead of individual file writes. This avoids tool echo overhead (~7,500 tokens saved).
+
+## Core Skills (generate at setup)
+
+These 5 skills are needed immediately after setup:
+
+```
+.claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md    — company knowledge base
+.claude/skills/nurge-icp/SKILL.md        — ideal customer profile
+.claude/skills/nurge-personas/SKILL.md   — buyer personas
+.claude/skills/nurge-outbound/SKILL.md   — cold email drafting
+.claude/skills/nurge-pipeline/SKILL.md   — workflow orchestrator
+```
 
-Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Use the EXACT paths below. Do NOT use flat .md files. Do NOT rename skills.
+## Deferred Skills (generated on first use)
+
+These 11 skills are generated when the user first invokes them. Each skill's slash command should check if the SKILL.md exists; if not, generate it then using this guide + current config/data.
 
 ```
-.claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-icp/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-personas/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-research/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-qualify/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-pipeline/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-outbound/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-sequence/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-followup/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-replies/SKILL.md
@@ -25,7 +38,25 @@ Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Use the EXACT paths b
 .claude/skills/nurge-review/SKILL.md
 ```
 
-That is 16 skills. The 17th is nurge-setup (global, already installed at `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/SKILL.md` — do not regenerate it).
+## How to Write Skills
+
+Generate all core skills as a JSON array and pipe to `nurge skills write --stdin`:
+
+```bash
+echo '[
+  {"name": "product", "content": "---\nname: nurge-product\n..."},
+  {"name": "icp", "content": "---\nname: nurge-icp\n..."},
+  {"name": "personas", "content": "---\nname: nurge-personas\n..."},
+  {"name": "outbound", "content": "---\nname: nurge-outbound\n..."},
+  {"name": "pipeline", "content": "---\nname: nurge-pipeline\n..."}
+]' | nurge skills write --stdin
+```
+
+This writes all files and saves versions to the DB in one call. Output is a single summary line.
+
+## File Structure
+
+Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Do NOT use flat .md files. Do NOT rename skills. The `nurge skills write` command handles directory creation automatically.
 
 ## YAML Frontmatter

 # Skill Generation Guide
 
-This guide is used by all Phase 2 flows to generate the 16 project-local skills. Read this file when you reach the skill generation step (Z11, E14, O12, or S13).
+This guide is used by all Phase 2 flows to generate skills at setup (Z11, E14, O12, or S13).
 
-## File Structure
+## Context Efficiency
+
+**Generate only the 5 core skills at setup.** The rest are generated lazily on first use — this saves ~15,000 tokens of context during setup and produces better skills later (more data to personalize from).
+
+**Use `nurge skills write --stdin`** to write all skills in one CLI call instead of individual file writes. This avoids tool echo overhead (~7,500 tokens saved).
+
+## Core Skills (generate at setup)
+
+These 5 skills are needed immediately after setup:
+
+```
+.claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md    — company knowledge base
+.claude/skills/nurge-icp/SKILL.md        — ideal customer profile
+.claude/skills/nurge-personas/SKILL.md   — buyer personas
+.claude/skills/nurge-outbound/SKILL.md   — cold email drafting
+.claude/skills/nurge-pipeline/SKILL.md   — workflow orchestrator
+```
 
-Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Use the EXACT paths below. Do NOT use flat .md files. Do NOT rename skills.
+## Deferred Skills (generated on first use)
+
+These 11 skills are generated when the user first invokes them. Each skill's slash command should check if the SKILL.md exists; if not, generate it then using this guide + current config/data.
 
 ```
-.claude/skills/nurge-product/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-icp/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-personas/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-research/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-qualify/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-pipeline/SKILL.md
-.claude/skills/nurge-outbound/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-sequence/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-followup/SKILL.md
 .claude/skills/nurge-replies/SKILL.md
@@ -25,7 +38,25 @@ Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Use the EXACT paths b
 .claude/skills/nurge-review/SKILL.md
 ```
 
-That is 16 skills. The 17th is nurge-setup (global, already installed at `~/.claude/skills/nurge-setup/SKILL.md` — do not regenerate it).
+## How to Write Skills
+
+Generate all core skills as a JSON array and pipe to `nurge skills write --stdin`:
+
+```bash
+echo '[
+  {"name": "product", "content": "---\nname: nurge-product\n..."},
+  {"name": "icp", "content": "---\nname: nurge-icp\n..."},
+  {"name": "personas", "content": "---\nname: nurge-personas\n..."},
+  {"name": "outbound", "content": "---\nname: nurge-outbound\n..."},
+  {"name": "pipeline", "content": "---\nname: nurge-pipeline\n..."}
+]' | nurge skills write --stdin
+```
+
+This writes all files and saves versions to the DB in one call. Output is a single summary line.
+
+## File Structure
+
+Each skill MUST be a directory containing a SKILL.md file. Do NOT use flat .md files. Do NOT rename skills. The `nurge skills write` command handles directory creation automatically.
 
 ## YAML Frontmatter

content.text:
Here's what I see — a solid feature batch:

- **Metrics system** — CLI usage tracking with daily rollups + Convex sync
- **Scraper** — `nurge scrape <url>` for context-efficient website extraction
- **Email improvements** — `classify-auto` heuristics, `--compact` list flag
- **Skills batch write** — `nurge skills write --stdin` for one-call multi-skill generation
- **Setup optimization** — generate only 5 core skills at setup, defer 11; auto-classify before AI; use scraper instead of WebFetch

Next version would be **v0.1.3**. I'll commit all tracked + new source files (excluding `node_modules/`, `.claude/settings.local.json`, `research/`), push, and create a GitHub release.

Want me to proceed?

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: metrics tracking, website scraper, auto-classify, batch skill write, setup context optimization

- Add CLI usage metrics (internal/metrics) with daily rollups synced to Convex
- Add `nurge scrape <url>` for context-efficient website extraction during setup
- Add `nurge emails classify-auto` for heuristic pre-classification (internal→transactional→newsletter)
- Add `--compact` flag to `nurge emails list` (4 fields instead of 11)
- Add `nurge skills write --stdin` for batch skill file creation
- Optimize setup: generate only 5 core skills at setup, defer 11 to first use
- Setup skills now use scraper instead of WebFetch, auto-classify before AI classification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh release create v0.1.3 --title "v0.1.3" --notes "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What's New

### Metrics & Observability
- CLI usage tracking with daily rollups synced to Convex (`context_metrics_daily` table)
- `nurge metrics` command for viewing usage stats

### Website Scraper
- `nurge scrape <url>` — context-efficient website extraction for setup
- Returns structured JSON (title, description, headings, content, social proof, links)
- Setup now uses scraper instead of WebFetch, saving significant context

### Email Improvements
- `nurge emails classify-auto` — heuristic pre-classification handles ~60-70% of emails without AI (internal, transactional, newsletter, personal)
- `nurge emails list --compact` — returns only 4 fields instead of 11 for context efficiency

### Skill System
- `nurge skills write --stdin` — batch write multiple skills in one CLI call
- Setup now generates only 5 core skills; remaining 11 are deferred to first use (~15k tokens saved)

### Setup Optimization
- Auto-classify before AI classification
- Compact email listing during classification
- Batch skill writing instead of individual file writes
- Core-only skill generation at setup time
EOF
)"

content.text: Done. Pushed to main and released **v0.1.3**: https://github.com/usenurge/nurgesh/releases/tag/v0.1.3
